name: Autonomous Systems Laboratory
publications:
Smith, J.A., Jones, M.B. (2024). Multi-Agent Coordination in Dynamic Environments. IEEE Transactions on Robotics, 40(2), 345-360. DOI:10.1109/TRO.2024.001.

Lee, R.C., Smith, J.A. (2023). Scalable Cloud Orchestration for Autonomous Fleets. ACM SIGOPS, 12(1). DOI:10.1145/3580001.
grants: AI-Driven Autonomous Systems for Complex Environments
capabilities: Multi-robot coordination, reinforcement learning for control, simulation-to-real transfer, edge AI deployment, ROS 2 development, human-robot interaction research.
equipment: NVIDIA DGX A100 workstation (320GB GPU memory), fleet of 6 TurtleBot4 mobile robots, 2 Franka Panda robot arms, Intel RealSense depth camera array, motion capture system (10-camera Vicon), high-performance compute cluster (64 cores, 512GB RAM).
personnel: 2 postdoctoral researchers, 4 PhD students, 3 MS students, 2 undergraduate research assistants.
safety_requirements: All personnel must complete robot safety training (online module + in-person orientation) before operating equipment. High-voltage equipment certification required for certain experiments. Emergency stop procedures posted in lab.

name: Cybersecurity Research Group
publications: Jones, M.B. (2024). Adversarial Attacks on Industrial Control Systems. IEEE S&P 2024. Proceedings of the IEEE Symposium on Security and Privacy.
grants: Cybersecurity Education Pathways for Underrepresented Students
capabilities: Network protocol analysis, penetration testing, malware reverse engineering, ICS/SCADA security, cryptographic protocol design, security education.
equipment: Dedicated isolated network lab (20 nodes), hardware security modules, FPGA development boards, packet capture infrastructure.
safety_requirements: All research conducted on isolated networks. No testing on production or live systems. IRB approval required for human-subjects studies.
